content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed `origin/master...HEAD` excluding `.ralph-burning/` orchestration state and read the new fixture/mocks end-to-end. I found three fix-now issues: the persisted milestone bundle and `.beads` graph are not topologically consistent, mock mutations do not keep filesystem/BV state in sync, and the mock BR command matcher is too narrow to back the real create/close flows.

## Amendments

1. [src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:207-266,277-357] The fixture persists a `plan.json` where `ms-e2e-scenario.root-epic` is a normal bead proposal inside a workstream, then hand-builds `.beads/issues.jsonl` by reinterpreting dependencies on that same id as `parent_child`. That is not a topology the real exporter can produce: production materialization creates a separate milestone root plus workstream epics and treats `proposal.depends_on` as blocking edges (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:1602-1757`). As written, the plan artifacts and bead graph describe different worlds, so integration tests can pass against an impossible state. Remediation: make the bundle and bead graph derive from the same production topology, or stop reusing the same bead id for both a proposal and the exported root epic.
   Rationale: This fixture is supposed to be a coherent end-to-end workspace. If the milestone plan and `.beads` graph cannot correspond to the same real export, tests built on it will validate the wrong invariants.
2. [src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:109-148,389-400,613-621] The mock mutation path only updates private in-memory state. `create_follow_up`/`close_follow_up` mutate `created_follow_ups`, `list`/`show` read from that vector, but `.beads/issues.jsonl` on disk is never rewritten and `MockBvAdapter` captures a fixed `--robot-next` response at construction time. After a close, the filesystem still shows the original open graph and `bv --robot-next` still recommends `ms-e2e-scenario.task-prepare-workspace` instead of advancing to the newly ready bead. That makes the fixture internally inconsistent for reconciliation/controller tests that combine br mutations with filesystem or BV reads. Remediation: share mutable scenario state across both mocks and rewrite the on-disk bead graph / next-bead selection from that state when mutations occur.
   Rationale: The intended end-to-end success path closes a bead, syncs, and then captures the next-step BV hint (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:257-319`). With the current fixture, those post-mutation reads observe stale state.
3. [src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:555-578] `ScenarioBrMock::response_for_call` only accepts one literal create shape (`br create --title=Follow-up validation bead --type=task --priority=2`) and one literal close reason (`--reason=Fixture cleanup`). The real code paths this fixture is supposed to support use different commands: success reconciliation closes with `task {task_id} completed successfully` (`src/contexts/automation_runtime/success_reconciliation.rs:478-480`), and proposed-bead creation goes through `BrMutationAdapter::create_bead`, which can add `--labels` and `--description` (`src/adapters/br_process.rs:1336-1359`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:4582-4590`). Wiring this fixture into the real mutation adapters will exhaust the mock instead of exercising the intended workflow. Remediation: match the production command shapes rather than a single smoke-test literal.
   Rationale: A scenario fixture for integration testing needs to tolerate the argument forms emitted by the real adapters. Right now it only supports the bespoke commands used in its own smoke test.
